rationale_for_baseline_tier: The patient is systemically stable (subjective low-grade fever only, no rigors, no measured haemodynamic compromise), so this is neither a 000 (T5) nor an immediate-ED-by-ambulance situation. But an ACUTE HOT SWOLLEN JOINT cannot be distinguished from septic arthritis or disseminated gonococcal infection on history and video alone, and telehealth cannot aspirate the joint — the one test that excludes infection. The safe disposition is therefore SAME-DAY in-person assessment (urgent GP / urgent care / ED as locally appropriate) for joint aspiration: T3. This is the narrow, justified telehealth uplift — a plausible serious mimic that genuinely cannot be excluded remotely and would need urgent in-person management if present. Reassuring the patient remotely and deferring (T1/T2) would be a critical under-triage; dispatching a 000 ambulance for a stable patient (T5) would be over-triage.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The acute hot swollen joint itself — present now and not excludable as infected via telehealth
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day in-person assessment for joint aspiration
escalation_edges.rationale: Every acute hot joint must be aspirated to exclude septic arthritis before it is attributed to a reactive process; this cannot wait for a routine appointment and cannot be done remotely.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because your ankle is hot, swollen and came on suddenly, we can't safely rule out a joint infection over a call — you need to be seen in person today so the joint can be examined and, if needed, a sample taken. I'll help you arrange that now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Joint becomes fiery-red, tense and increasingly hot, or you develop a high fever with shaking chills/rigors
escalation_edges.rationale: These signal an established septic joint and/or bacteraemia — a surgical and medical emergency needing urgent drainage and IV antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Call 000 straight away if the ankle turns fiery red, tense and even hotter, or if you get a high fever with uncontrollable shaking chills — that could mean a serious joint infection in the bloodstream.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New deep eye pain, sensitivity to light, or any blurring or loss of vision
escalation_edges.rationale: These suggest vision-threatening acute anterior uveitis rather than the benign gritty conjunctivitis present now, and need same-day ophthalmology.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your eyes become painful, very sensitive to light, or your vision blurs or drops, go to an emergency department or eye clinic the same day — that needs urgent specialist eye assessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone and is unable to stand for long or weight-bear safely on the ankle
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Confirm the patient can physically get to same-day assessment; arrange transport help if needed rather than assuming they can self-transport, and ensure they are not left without a way to escalate if they deteriorate at home alone.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person today so your ankle can be examined and infection ruled out — please arrange an urgent same-day appointment (urgent GP, urgent care, or an emergency department if that's the quickest option). In the meantime, take an anti-inflammatory with food, rest the joint, and use lubricating eye drops for the gritty eyes.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person assessment; call 000 if the emergency signs below develop
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours, use an after-hours GP service, urgent care, or the emergency department — do not wait until the next in-hours period for the joint to be assessed.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide a summary of the reactive triad (recent Campylobacter enteritis, asymmetric ankle oligoarthritis, non-purulent conjunctivitis, urethritis), the telehealth limitation (joint not examined/aspirated), and the plan for same-day aspiration, STI screen and inflammatory bloods.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as a benign reactive-arthritis flare manageable at home / routine GP, deferring in-person joint assessment.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A septic joint or disseminated gonococcal infection is missed and destroys the joint or causes sepsis over the days before routine review.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Booking a GP appointment in 24-48 hours rather than same-day assessment for an acute hot joint — a delay of a day or two in aspirating a possibly septic joint risks irreversible cartilage damage.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Dispatching a 000 emergency ambulance for a systemically stable patient who needs same-day in-person joint assessment, not resuscitation — unnecessary ambulance use, far less harmful than under-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the acute hot joint is a sterile reactive arthritis or a septic arthritis / disseminated gonococcal infection — indistinguishable without aspiration, which telehealth cannot perform.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Hold the serious infective possibilities open, arrange same-day in-person assessment for aspiration, give interim symptom advice and explicit safety-netting — do not collapse onto the benign diagnosis remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Pattern-matching the reactive triad, reassuring the patient it is a benign flare, and deferring or omitting the in-person joint assessment.
